strategy.name: Michaël van de Poppe — Mean Reversion + Macro Rotation Framework
strategy.description: A macro-driven mean-reversion framework for Bitcoin (and Ethereum). Van de Poppe uses the 21-day MA as the 'fair price' anchor: buy when price is significantly below the 21-day MA (mean reversion long), sell when price is stretched above it. Macro context is set by Bitcoin vs Gold RSI on weekly/bi-weekly timeframes. When BTC/GOLD weekly RSI < 30, it historically marks a cycle bottom — maximum accumulation zone. When gold peaks and starts to consolidate, Bitcoin rotation begins. Day trading variant: grid/volatility strategy buying dips below 20-day MA and selling 1% above, targeting 30-50% APR.

indicators.21day_ma: Primary trend filter and fair-price anchor. Price below 21-day MA = bearish structure; price breaking above 21-day MA = bull cycle resumption trigger. Van de Poppe calls this the single most important level to watch for Bitcoin on the daily chart.
indicators.50day_ma: Secondary trend level. Price must clear both 21-day and 50-day MAs for full bullish confirmation. Also used as a support/resistance zone during pullbacks.
indicators.200day_or_200week_ma: Long-term macro support. The 200-week MA acts as the absolute bear-market floor; price touching it confirms cycle-bottom territory.
indicators.50week_ma: Bull/bear regime divider on the weekly chart. Sustained close above 50-week MA = bull market confirmed. Sustained close below = bear market. Sitting at ~100K in the current cycle.
indicators.20day_ma_grid: Fair-price reference for the grid/volatility day-trading strategy. Buy below, sell above. Identical in function to the 21-day MA but used specifically for short-term grid orders.
indicators.btc_vs_gold_rsi_weekly: Primary macro accumulation signal. Weekly RSI of BTC/GOLD pair. When < 30: historically marks the best accumulation window for the entire cycle. Readings of 22 (April 2026) are the lowest ever recorded. When > 70: overvalued vs gold, consider reducing Bitcoin.
indicators.eth_vs_silver_rsi: Ethereum-specific accumulation signal. Bi-weekly and weekly RSI of ETH/SILVER. When at all-time-low RSI levels: Ethereum is deeply undervalued vs silver — strong accumulation trigger.
indicators.macd_3day: Momentum exhaustion indicator on the 3-day timeframe. When 3-day MACD is at historical lows (below all prior readings), it signals a capitulation bottom — strongest buy signal. When 3-day MACD makes new highs, reduce risk.
indicators.macd_weekly: Weekly momentum. Used alongside 3-day MACD to confirm macro turning points. Historically extreme lows on weekly MACD have corresponded with major bear-market bottoms.
indicators.fear_greed_index: Sentiment extremes filter. Readings at or below levels seen in 2022 (bare market lows) = strong buy zone. Readings near 100 = euphoria, reduce exposure.
indicators.rsi_daily_btc: Daily RSI on Bitcoin. Lowest daily RSI ever recorded (previously only seen at COVID bottom and 2018 bottom) = high-conviction buy signal. RSI < 30 on daily = short-term oversold, look for entries.
indicators.business_cycle_indicator: Macro regime context. When business cycle is at its weakest (PMI/ISM data weakening, unemployment rising, Fed pivoting to cuts) = best time to step into crypto. When business cycle peaks = reduce crypto allocation.
indicators.realized_price: On-chain fair value. BTC trading near or below realized price = accumulation zone confirmed by on-chain fundamentals.
indicators.btc_production_cost: Mining cost floor. ~$70K in current cycle. Bitcoin trading at or below production cost is a strong fundamental buy signal.
indicators.gold_consolidation_signal: Rotation trigger. When gold starts to consolidate or correct after a parabolic run (extreme weekly/bi-weekly MACD on gold), Bitcoin rotation begins. Watch for gold MACD to peak and roll over.
indicators.choppiness_index_14: Market regime filter (period 14). CI < 50 = trending market, trade allowed. CI > 50 = choppy/sideways market, skip trade. Inspired by David (Trading with DaviddTech) who used this to cut max drawdown by 5%+ by avoiding false signals in choppy conditions. The CI filters out the most damaging trades — those taken when the market has no clear direction.

bullish: BTC/GOLD weekly RSI is below 30 (currently at historic lows ~22) — cycle accumulation window confirmed
3-day MACD on Bitcoin is at or near historical lows (heavier than 2022 or COVID crash readings)
Daily RSI on Bitcoin is at extreme lows (comparable to COVID bottom or 2018 crash bottom)
Price is at or has just broken above the 21-day MA after being suppressed below it for months
Gold/Silver weekly MACD is showing exhaustion or topping signals — rotation into Bitcoin imminent
Business cycle is weakening (rising unemployment, falling PMI, Fed moving toward rate cuts) — new cycle starting
Fear and greed index is at levels last seen at 2022 bare-market lows
ETH/SILVER bi-weekly RSI is at all-time lows — Ethereum deeply undervalued vs commodities
Bitcoin is trading near realized price (~$50K) or production cost (~$70K) — on-chain fundamental floor
bearish: Price is below the 21-day MA and has failed to reclaim it for multiple weeks
3-day MACD on Bitcoin is making new highs or extremely extended to the upside
BTC/GOLD weekly RSI is above 70 — Bitcoin overvalued vs gold
Gold weekly MACD is at historic highs and accelerating — commodities in euphoria phase, Bitcoin lags
Business cycle is at peak (high PMI, low unemployment, rising rates) — risk-off environment
Fear and greed index is near 100 — euphoria, late-cycle signal
Bitcoin failed to break above 50-week MA (currently ~$100K) and rejected back below it
neutral: Price is consolidating around the 21-day MA without a clear directional break
BTC/GOLD weekly RSI is between 30 and 50 — not at extremes in either direction
Gold is consolidating after a big run but has not yet topped — rotation window approaching but not confirmed
Business cycle is mid-cycle — no strong signal in either direction

long: PRIMARY SIGNAL: BTC/GOLD weekly RSI crosses below 30 — begin accumulation. The lower the RSI goes below 30, the more aggressive the accumulation.
CONFIRMATION 1: 3-day MACD on Bitcoin is at or below historical extremes (heavier than 2022 or COVID crash) — add to position.
CONFIRMATION 2: Daily RSI on Bitcoin drops to extreme lows (sub-20 level, comparable to COVID bottom or 2018 bottom) — maximum accumulation trigger.

TREND ENTRY: Price breaks and closes above the 21-day MA on the daily chart after being below it for an extended period — enter a trend-following long. First target: 21-day MA breakout level, then prior resistance zone (e.g. $92K then $100-105K).

GRID/VOLATILITY ENTRY: Price drops below the 20-day MA. Place buy orders in a grid below the 20-day MA spaced according to the asset's average daily volatility (BTC avg ~2.24%/day). Scale in deeper as price moves further below the mean. Target +1% per grid cell above the 20-day MA.

MEAN REVERSION ENTRY: When a 6-sigma or larger single-day crash occurs (e.g., $10K single-day drop in BTC), treat as capitulation. Begin accumulating immediately — these outlier moves historically snap back toward fair value (20-day MA) within days to weeks.

MACRO ROTATION ENTRY: When gold weekly MACD peaks and starts to roll over after an extreme bull run, AND BTC/GOLD RSI is below 30, begin building a Bitcoin long position in anticipation of the gold-to-Bitcoin rotation trade.
short: Van de Poppe does NOT advocate directional shorting of crypto. He uses leverage only for delta-neutral hedging of existing long positions.

HEDGE ONLY: If holding a long Bitcoin/ETH position and the 21-day MA is failing as support, open a short hedge on BTC or ETH (the only two assets OKX/platforms support for short margin) to reduce net delta — not to profit from downside.

REDUCE EXPOSURE: When BTC/GOLD weekly RSI is above 70 AND 3-day MACD is making new historic highs AND fear/greed index is near 100 — reduce spot position size. Do not hold full allocation into euphoria peaks.

SELL GRID ORDERS: In the grid/volatility strategy, place sell orders 1% above each buy order level. When price rises above the 20-day MA, sell orders execute automatically. This is not a directional short — it is taking profit on long positions.

exit_rules: GRID TAKE PROFIT: In grid/volatility strategy, each buy order has a corresponding sell order placed 1% above it. Profit target per trade is 1%. Reinvest profits to compound. Target 30-50% APR.

TREND POSITION TAKE PROFIT: When price has broken above 21-day MA and approaches prior resistance zones, begin scaling out. First scale: at prior resistance (e.g., $92K). Second scale: at round numbers / psychological levels (e.g., $100K, $105K). Final scale: when BTC/GOLD weekly RSI approaches 70 or above.

ACCUMULATION POSITION EXIT: Cycle-top signal is when gold peaks and starts strongly underperforming Bitcoin, Bitcoin/GOLD RSI approaches extreme highs (70+), and fear/greed index reaches 100. Rotate back toward gold or commodities.

STOP LOSS — TREND TRADE: If price breaks back below the 21-day MA after a bullish breakout entry, the thesis is invalidated. Exit or significantly reduce the trend-following long. Next major support: 50-day MA.

STOP LOSS — MACRO ACCUMULATION: If Bitcoin fails to hold realized price (~$50K) AND production cost (~$70K) AND 200-week MA — reduce accumulation pace and reassess. A sustained break below all three levels would indicate a deeper bear market than historical precedent.

DO NOT SELL AT FEAR EXTREMES: Van de Poppe explicitly states not to sell when daily RSI is at historic lows, 3-day MACD is at historic lows, and BTC/GOLD RSI is below 30 simultaneously — this is the worst possible time to exit.

ROTATION EXIT: When gold and silver MACD rolls over from historic highs, and Bitcoin starts to outperform, begin exiting gold/commodity positions and entering Bitcoin. The inverse rotation (Bitcoin to gold) applies when BTC/GOLD RSI peaks above 70.
risk_rules: No directional leverage for long-only speculation. Leverage is reserved for delta-neutral hedging of spot positions only.

Grid/volatility strategy: allocate only a portion of total portfolio (van de Poppe uses a separate 'day trading portfolio' starting at $1K, scaled to $10-17K). Keep grid allocation separate from core accumulation holdings.

Core accumulation position sizing: DCA monthly regardless of price direction during macro accumulation windows (BTC/GOLD RSI < 30). Van de Poppe adds $5-10K per month to altcoin/BTC positions during confirmed accumulation phases.
During accumulation: prefer larger-cap assets first (Bitcoin, Ethereum) before moving to altcoins. Capital flows into larger market caps first.

Grid strategy fund management: never become fully allocated in the grid. When price drops below all grid levels, remove top sell orders and re-place them lower to maintain volume generation. The strategy requires generating trades continuously.

Pair selection for grid: start with one pair. Bitcoin is lower volatility (avg 2.24%/day) and lower risk. Higher-volatility altcoins offer higher grid returns but require smaller position sizes. Optimize grid spacing to the specific pair's daily volatility.
On extreme crashes (6-sigma events): do not panic sell. These outlier moves are historically followed by mean reversion to fair price (20-day MA) within days to weeks.
Do not chase gold and silver in euphoria phase — they are at 7+ sigma events in 2025/2026. The opportunity is in what has NOT moved yet (Bitcoin, Ethereum).
Avoid increasing altcoin risk during early accumulation phase. Establish Bitcoin and Ethereum first, then rotate into altcoins once momentum confirms.
avoid_trade_conditions: Do not enter a trend-following long while price is still below the 21-day MA with no sign of reclaiming it.
Do not buy if BTC/GOLD weekly RSI is above 70 — Bitcoin is overvalued vs gold at those levels.
Do not add to altcoin positions when the trend on the weekly or daily chart is still decisively down (lower highs, lower lows structure intact).
Do not use leverage to increase upside speculation — only use leverage for hedging existing positions.
Do not FOMO into gold or silver when weekly/bi-weekly MACD is at historic highs — rotation away from commodities is imminent.
Avoid buying assets that are still making new lows without any bullish divergence on RSI or MACD.
Do not trade the grid strategy on extremely illiquid or low-volume altcoins — requires consistent bid/ask spread and sufficient volume to fill 1,000-2,000 trades per month.

CHOPPINESS FILTER: Do not enter any trade when the 14-period Choppiness Index is above 50. CI > 50 means the market is in a sideways/choppy regime — entries in this environment produce the worst risk/reward ratio and historically cause the most drawdown.

timeframes.primary_bias: Weekly (1W) — BTC/GOLD RSI, 50-week MA, weekly MACD for macro regime and accumulation signals
timeframes.trend_entry: Daily (1D) — 21-day MA and 50-day MA for trend entry/exit triggers; daily RSI for extreme oversold conditions
timeframes.grid_trading: 3-day (3D) — 3-day MACD for identifying capitulation extremes; 3-day candles for medium-term momentum
timeframes.grid_execution: Intraday (1H or less) — Grid/volatility orders execute continuously on hourly or shorter timeframes; van de Poppe executes 1,000-2,000 trades per month

grid_strategy_parameters.description: Van de Poppe's active grid/volatility day trading strategy, executed on OKX or Hyperliquid
grid_strategy_parameters.fair_price_reference: 20-day MA (called 'the mean' — buy below it, sell above it)
grid_strategy_parameters.btc_daily_volatility_reference: 2.24% average daily move — use this to space grid levels
grid_strategy_parameters.grid_spacing_logic: Space buy orders at intervals proportional to the asset's average daily volatility. The further price moves below the 20-day MA, the more allocation deployed.
grid_strategy_parameters.allocation_rule: Scale allocation deeper as price moves further from the 20-day MA. When price is near the mean, reduce grid density. When price is far below, increase grid density.
grid_strategy_parameters.example_return: 36% APR on ETH/EUR grid; up to 41% APR on more aggressive settings
grid_strategy_parameters.reinvestment: Reinvest profits into grid to compound returns
grid_strategy_parameters.supported_platforms: OKX (spot grid bots, spot DCA bots, spot margin up to 10x long), Hyperliquid

watchlist_notes.BTCUSD: Primary asset. Watch daily 21-day MA breakout. Monitor BTC/GOLD weekly RSI. Core accumulation asset.
watchlist_notes.ETHUSD: Secondary asset. Watch ETH/SILVER bi-weekly RSI for all-time-low signals. Ethereum follows Bitcoin but with higher leverage in bull markets.
watchlist_notes.BTCUSD_vs_GOLD: Most important relative-value chart. Weekly RSI < 30 = buy Bitcoin aggressively. RSI > 70 = reduce Bitcoin, consider gold.
watchlist_notes.ETHUSD_vs_SILVER: Ethereum-specific relative-value chart. Bi-weekly RSI at all-time lows = maximum ETH accumulation signal.
watchlist_notes.GOLD: Rotation indicator. When gold MACD peaks from historic highs, expect Bitcoin rotation. Monitor weekly MACD for exhaustion signals.
watchlist_notes.SILVER: Correlated to ETH via ETH/SILVER ratio. Silver euphoria (7+ sigma weekly moves) = ETH rotation opportunity imminent.

notes: Michaël van de Poppe (@CryptoMichNL) operates two distinct strategy layers: (1) MACRO ACCUMULATION — a multi-month, macro-driven DCA strategy using BTC/GOLD RSI on weekly timeframes as the primary signal. When BTC/GOLD weekly RSI < 30 (historically 22 in April 2026 — lowest ever), it is the strongest possible accumulation signal. He explicitly bought aggressively during this window. (2) GRID/VOLATILITY TRADING — an active day-trading strategy using the 20-day MA as fair price, buying below it and selling 1% above it, targeting 30-50% APR. He runs this on Hyperliquid and OKX using spot grid bots with 1,000-2,000 trades per month. Current view (April 2026): Bitcoin is at its lowest valuation vs gold ever (RSI ~22), 3-day MACD is at historic lows, daily RSI is at historic lows — all three signals align for maximum accumulation. Key resistance levels: 21-day MA, then $92K, then $100-105K (50-week MA). Gold has topped after a massive run; Bitcoin rotation is next, following the pattern seen in 2016 and 2020. Van de Poppe does NOT use directional shorts. He does NOT do altcoin speculation during early recovery — establishes Bitcoin and ETH first.
